title: haplopappus acradenius subsp. eremophilus
intro: Haplopappus acradenius subsp. eremophilus is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), commonly found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant, also known as Erigeron trilobus, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Haplopappus acradenius subsp. eremophilus include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and viruses. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make topical applications, such as salves and creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
